message:
Add CompactionResult struct for pool compaction ID remapping

Introduce CompactionResult to provide callers with ID remapping data
after a compaction operation. When the pool compacts, existing GraphemeIds
become invalid and must be updated to their new values.

CompactionResult fields:
- old_to_new: HashMap<u32, u32> mapping old pool IDs to new pool IDs
- slots_freed: Number of free slots removed during compaction
- bytes_saved: Estimated memory reclaimed

CompactionResult methods:
- has_remappings(): Check if any IDs were actually remapped
- remap(old_id): Look up new ID for a given old ID

The typical usage pattern (shown in doc example):
1. Call compact() which returns CompactionResult
2. Iterate through all stored GraphemeIds
3. Remap each ID using old_to_new lookup
4. Rebuild GraphemeId with new pool_id but same width

IDs not found in old_to_new were either:
- Invalid before compaction
- Already freed (refcount reached 0)
- Unchanged (not moved during compaction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
